Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 12:32 ET
Dispersion trade signals flash as index puts meet single-name call euphoria
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Index P/C at 1.30 versus single-name 0.68 creates textbook dispersion setup into GDP data
  • 2. Implied correlation crashes to 0.05 while dealer positive gamma dampens index moves
  • 3. UNH unusual two-way flow amid IV trading 20% cheap suggests volatility mispricing
NARRATIVE — dispersion opportunity

The most compelling setup emerges from the extreme P/C divergence — index puts trading 1.30 while single-names show call-heavy 0.68, creating a classic institutional dispersion hedge into this week's GDP release. Implied correlation at 0.05 confirms stock-specific regime dominance, with single-name volatility more efficiently priced than index vol. This divergence amplifies when combined with dealer positive gamma of $64.3M on SPY, which will dampen index moves while individual names retain full volatility expression. UNH's extraordinary two-way flow — calls +1269% and puts +1070% — occurs while the stock trades IV 20% cheap versus its sector ETF, suggesting volatility mispricing ahead of potential healthcare policy developments. The magnitude of two-way interest typically signals either acquisition speculation or major fundamental catalyst positioning. Similarly, NVTS shows massive call flow (+1252%) paired with significant put activity (+422%), indicating vol buyers are positioning for binary outcomes rather than directional bets. Tech names reveal concerning divergences despite surface-level strength. XLK shows "tech bull/skew fear" tension while AAPL exhibits "vol high/skew flat" — suggesting institutional caution beneath retail call buying. With Millennium's massive MSFT build (+135% in Q4) contrasting Druckenmiller's complete META exit, smart money positioning remains fractured ahead of Wednesday's GDP print.

Layer 1: Sentiment +43Layer 2: GEX +$64.3MLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.30/0.68Layer 4: IC 0.05
